blocks.text: Of Simple Mercantile Defraudment generally attended to: the multitude will be always governed by appearances. The case is therefore that in some instances the rich must be punished more than otherwise they need, lest the poor should think themselves entitled to complain. were better should be shewn in an oblique and inexplicit manner. This is done by annexing the favour rather to the offence than to the offender.
